oai_dc:dc.dc:title: Effects of propylene glycol on milk yield in multiparous cows based on early lactation rumination and eating behavior: A randomized controlled trial
oai_dc:dc.dc:creator: Davis, Sara
Dorella, Marina
Florentino, Cainan
Ruch, Megan
Shepley, Elise
Caixeta, Luciano
oai_dc:dc.dc:description: In a shrinking workforce with increasing demands for labor ef­ficiency, it’s essential to target treatments to cows most likely to benefit. Although propylene glycol (PG) is commonly used to treat hyperketonemia (HYK), prior research has shown con­flicting results. Early lactation rumination and eating behavior may help identify cows in need of treatment. This study evalu­ated the effect of PG treatment on milk yield in cows with vary­ing levels of early postpartum rumination and eating time.
oai_dc:dc.dc:source: American Association of Bovine Practitioners Conference Proceedings; Vol. 58 No. 2 (2025): 2025 AABP Annual Conference Proceedings; 259
oai_dc:dc.dc:rights: Copyright (c) 2026 American Association of Bovine Practitioners
